verses.text: By the sea He teaches in parables: 'Behold, a sower went forth to sow.' Earthly stories. Christ wraps heavenly truth in homely pictures.
By the sea He teaches in parables: 'Behold, a sower went forth to sow.' Earthly stories. Christ wraps heavenly truth in homely pictures.
By the sea He teaches in parables: 'Behold, a sower went forth to sow.' Earthly stories. Christ wraps heavenly truth in homely pictures.
Some seed fell by the wayside and on stony ground, soon withering. Shallow hearing. The careless and the shallow lose the word that falls on them.
Some seed fell by the wayside and on stony ground, soon withering. Shallow hearing. The careless and the shallow lose the word that falls on them.
Some seed fell by the wayside and on stony ground, soon withering. Shallow hearing. The careless and the shallow lose the word that falls on them.
Some fell among thorns, choked; some on good ground, bearing fruit; 'who hath ears... let him hear.' Fruitful soil. The honest heart yields a harvest from the word.
Some fell among thorns, choked; some on good ground, bearing fruit; 'who hath ears... let him hear.' Fruitful soil. The honest heart yields a harvest from the word.
Some fell among thorns, choked; some on good ground, bearing fruit; 'who hath ears... let him hear.' Fruitful soil. The honest heart yields a harvest from the word.
He speaks in parables, for 'to him that hath shall be given,' but from him that hath not shall be taken. Hidden and revealed. The word both enlightens the willing and veils the careless.
He speaks in parables, for 'to him that hath shall be given,' but from him that hath not shall be taken. Hidden and revealed. The word both enlightens the willing and veils the careless.
He speaks in parables, for 'to him that hath shall be given,' but from him that hath not shall be taken. Hidden and revealed. The word both enlightens the willing and veils the careless.
He speaks in parables, for 'to him that hath shall be given,' but from him that hath not shall be taken. Hidden and revealed. The word both enlightens the willing and veils the careless.
In them Isaiah is fulfilled, hearing they hear not; 'blessed are your eyes, for they see.' A great privilege. To understand God's truth is a gift the prophets longed for.
In them Isaiah is fulfilled, hearing they hear not; 'blessed are your eyes, for they see.' A great privilege. To understand God's truth is a gift the prophets longed for.
In them Isaiah is fulfilled, hearing they hear not; 'blessed are your eyes, for they see.' A great privilege. To understand God's truth is a gift the prophets longed for.
In them Isaiah is fulfilled, hearing they hear not; 'blessed are your eyes, for they see.' A great privilege. To understand God's truth is a gift the prophets longed for.
The seed is the word; the wayside hearer loses it to the wicked one; the stony hearer believes for a while. No root. Quick joy without depth soon fades.
The seed is the word; the wayside hearer loses it to the wicked one; the stony hearer believes for a while. No root. Quick joy without depth soon fades.
The seed is the word; the wayside hearer loses it to the wicked one; the stony hearer believes for a while. No root. Quick joy without depth soon fades.
The thorny hearer is choked by cares and riches; the good ground hears and bears thirty, sixty, a hundredfold. Lasting fruit. The fruitful heart receives and holds the word.
The thorny hearer is choked by cares and riches; the good ground hears and bears thirty, sixty, a hundredfold. Lasting fruit. The fruitful heart receives and holds the word.
The thorny hearer is choked by cares and riches; the good ground hears and bears thirty, sixty, a hundredfold. Lasting fruit. The fruitful heart receives and holds the word.
The kingdom is like a man who sowed good seed, but an enemy sowed tares among the wheat. Mixed field. Good and evil grow side by side in the world.
The kingdom is like a man who sowed good seed, but an enemy sowed tares among the wheat. Mixed field. Good and evil grow side by side in the world.
The kingdom is like a man who sowed good seed, but an enemy sowed tares among the wheat. Mixed field. Good and evil grow side by side in the world.
Let both grow till the harvest, lest the wheat be uprooted; then gather the tares to burn. Patient delay. God allows the wicked to remain until His appointed reckoning.
Let both grow till the harvest, lest the wheat be uprooted; then gather the tares to burn. Patient delay. God allows the wicked to remain until His appointed reckoning.
Let both grow till the harvest, lest the wheat be uprooted; then gather the tares to burn. Patient delay. God allows the wicked to remain until His appointed reckoning.
Let both grow till the harvest, lest the wheat be uprooted; then gather the tares to burn. Patient delay. God allows the wicked to remain until His appointed reckoning.
The kingdom is like a mustard seed, least yet becoming a tree, and like leaven hidden in meal. Small but mighty. God's kingdom grows from tiny beginnings to fill the earth.
The kingdom is like a mustard seed, least yet becoming a tree, and like leaven hidden in meal. Small but mighty. God's kingdom grows from tiny beginnings to fill the earth.
The kingdom is like a mustard seed, least yet becoming a tree, and like leaven hidden in meal. Small but mighty. God's kingdom grows from tiny beginnings to fill the earth.
All this He spoke in parables, fulfilling, 'I will utter things... kept secret.' Veiled truth. Parables both reveal and conceal, according to the heart.
All this He spoke in parables, fulfilling, 'I will utter things... kept secret.' Veiled truth. Parables both reveal and conceal, according to the heart.
He explains the tares: the good seed are the children of the kingdom, the tares of the wicked one, sown by the devil. Two seeds. The field of the world holds children of God and of evil.
He explains the tares: the good seed are the children of the kingdom, the tares of the wicked one, sown by the devil. Two seeds. The field of the world holds children of God and of evil.
He explains the tares: the good seed are the children of the kingdom, the tares of the wicked one, sown by the devil. Two seeds. The field of the world holds children of God and of evil.
He explains the tares: the good seed are the children of the kingdom, the tares of the wicked one, sown by the devil. Two seeds. The field of the world holds children of God and of evil.
As tares are burned, so at the end the angels gather the wicked; 'then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun.' Final separation. The harvest divides the saved from the lost for ever.
As tares are burned, so at the end the angels gather the wicked; 'then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun.' Final separation. The harvest divides the saved from the lost for ever.
As tares are burned, so at the end the angels gather the wicked; 'then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun.' Final separation. The harvest divides the saved from the lost for ever.
As tares are burned, so at the end the angels gather the wicked; 'then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun.' Final separation. The harvest divides the saved from the lost for ever.
The kingdom is like treasure hid in a field; a man sells all to buy it. Worth everything. Christ's kingdom is worth the surrender of all we have.
Like a merchant who finds 'one pearl of great price' and sells all to buy it. Supreme value. The wise give up everything to gain Christ.
Like a merchant who finds 'one pearl of great price' and sells all to buy it. Supreme value. The wise give up everything to gain Christ.
The kingdom is like a net gathering all kinds; at the end the bad are cast away. Gospel net. The visible church holds good and bad until the final sorting.
The kingdom is like a net gathering all kinds; at the end the bad are cast away. Gospel net. The visible church holds good and bad until the final sorting.
The kingdom is like a net gathering all kinds; at the end the bad are cast away. Gospel net. The visible church holds good and bad until the final sorting.

Understanding these, every instructed scribe brings forth treasure 'new and old.' Furnished teacher. The taught disciple shares both old truths and fresh insight.
Understanding these, every instructed scribe brings forth treasure 'new and old.' Furnished teacher. The taught disciple shares both old truths and fresh insight.
Understanding these, every instructed scribe brings forth treasure 'new and old.' Furnished teacher. The taught disciple shares both old truths and fresh insight.
In His own country they are offended — 'is not this the carpenter's son?' — and He does few works there for their unbelief. Familiarity blinds. Unbelief shuts the door on Christ's power.
